themes.exampleQuote: A perfect speakeasy vibe with the best cocktails I've had in Pittsburgh.
The drinks were amazing, the atmosphere was elegant, and the service was top notch.
Alicia was one of the best bartenders I've had — definitely deserves the bar manager position.
We were informed that we had to leave because there is a 90-minute time limit. This policy is only mentioned in the menu in very small print.
I have tried to get in a few times but it is always super packed.

notable_quote: I will be making the drive from Cleveland JUST for this bar in the near future.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers are consistent about the moody botanical aesthetic, the inventive cocktails, and individual bartenders by name — the one sustained grievance is a 90-minute seating limit that isn't communicated until it's too late.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-eight creators filmed Commerce Bar this quarter, generating 1.6 million TikTok plays — Commerce Bar posted none of it back. That gap is the editorial story of Spring 2026 for this East Liberty speakeasy: the city has effectively turned the place into a word-of-mouth engine, and the bar itself is running at near-zero on the channels that would let it capture any of that momentum. @andrewgardone's clip alone — captioned 'Best place in Pittsburgh it's slept on' — pulled 443,400 plays without a single owned asset to funnel the traffic anywhere useful, a problem compounded by the absence of a website or phone number on Google. Hidden Harbor holds the family lead this issue on social momentum, and the distance between them and Commerce Bar is almost entirely a distribution question, not a quality one. Closing that gap — even partially — is what moves this rank.
tldr_read: Massive TikTok creator footprint, almost no owned presence to catch it; Neighborhood Staples, #2 in Pittsburgh Bars.
tldr_meaning: 1.6 million external plays with no website, no TikTok account, and one Instagram post in 30 days means the ceiling on this rank is low until the business controls at least one outbound channel.

playbook.headline: Claim the TikTok the city already built
playbook.action: 28 creators posted 30+ videos generating 1.6M plays this quarter — create an owned TikTok account to anchor that traffic somewhere.

playbook.headline: Put a website and phone on Google
playbook.action: The Google Business profile has hours but no website and no phone number, cutting off the conversion path entirely for anyone landing there after a TikTok view.

playbook.headline: Post more than once a month on Instagram
playbook.action: One post in 30 days and a 0.72% engagement rate on a business account with clear visual material — the speakeasy aesthetic photographs itself, use it.
